scope: AgenC runs a one-shot startup migration that sets pragmatic personality only for existing users without explicit personality configuration.

requiredBehaviors: AgenC checks `.personality_migration` under the resolved AgenC home before any other migration work.
An existing marker returns `SkippedMarker` and does not write config.
Marker creation uses create-new semantics and writes exactly `v1\n`; an already-created marker is treated as success.
edgeCases: Skipped explicit-personality and no-session paths still create the marker.
The marker file is mode 0600 when the host honors file modes.

requiredBehaviors: A top-level configured personality returns `SkippedExplicitPersonality` and preserves the existing value.
The active startup profile's configured personality also returns `SkippedExplicitPersonality`.
edgeCases: Profile personalities are checked before recorded session lookup.
The skip path does not create a new top-level personality setting.

requiredBehaviors: AgenC lists active threads first and archived threads second using the resolved default provider.
No matching active or archived thread returns `SkippedNoSessions` and writes only the marker.
A recorded active or archived thread applies the migration.
edgeCases: Threads recorded under a different provider do not trigger migration.
AgenC uses the current project thread store because sessions are project-scoped.

requiredBehaviors: The applied path writes `personality = "pragmatic"` through AgenC's config edit builder.
Legacy JSON config is routed through existing config-file migrations before the edit is written to current TOML config.
The written config includes the current config file version and round-trips through AgenC's TOML parser.
edgeCases: Unsafe config migration skips block edits instead of overwriting ambiguous config.
Existing explicit personality values are never overwritten.

requiredBehaviors: Bootstrap runs config file migrations, then personality migration, then reloads config before resolving the first turn context when personality migration applies.
The startup provider/profile selection used for session detection matches the config/profile/env/CLI startup selection.
The first constructed turn context observes the migrated pragmatic personality.
edgeCases: No-session and explicit-personality statuses do not force an unnecessary config reload.
Bootstrap thread-store fallback provider uses the resolved startup provider for legacy records.

requiredBehaviors: The PR-10 verifier requires the migration module, config edit builder, bootstrap hook, parity matrix, and targeted tests.
The PR-10 verifier runs the focused migration and bootstrap tests.
edgeCases: Verify fails if the startup hook no longer calls the migration before turn context construction.
Verify fails if the marker or status names drift.
